authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions About California Senate Bill 478
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Attorney General is an authoritative consumer-protection enforcer, and this guidance directly addresses advertised pricing, mandatory fees, and restaurant examples relevant to guest complaints about service charges or hidden fees.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pricing disclosure review, guest-facing fee complaint handling, and policy-conflict escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for issuer-versus-merchant boundaries relevant to restaurant double-charge and refund-not-posted complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-status handling, and card-billing escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Menu and Vending Machines Labeling Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal authority for menu-labeling obligations at covered restaurant and similar retail food establishments, making this guidance authoritative for menu-item terminology, disclosure expectations, and covered-establishment boundaries in chain or franchise settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: menu-item disclosure review, guest-information handling, and chain-restaurant terminology grounding

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the recognized public baseline adopted or adapted by many state and local retail food regulators and is authoritative for restaurant food handling, time and temperature controls, contamination prevention, and corrective-action framing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety-sensitive complaint triage, unsafe-product stop conditions, and contamination-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Employee Health and Personal Hygiene Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA retail-food guidance is authoritative for employee illness, exclusion and restriction concepts, hygiene failures, and contamination-sensitive escalation behavior that can materially change the handling of a restaurant guest complaint.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: illness allegation review, contamination-sensitive support handling, and operational safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect refund rerouting, stored-value changes, and loyalty-account takeover handling in restaurant support operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch handling and fraud-sensitive support escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, location, guest context, order or booking identifiers, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, payment, pricing, and menu record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate checks, order state, tender history, refund status, fee presentation, promotion impacts, loyalty activity, and menu disclosures so the support answer stays tied to authoritative records.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Supported resolution and guest communication packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes and guest communication summaries that describe what is known, what was done, and what the guest must do next without overstating finality or authority.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, and repeat-failure escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported refund asks, discretionary recovery demands, repeat failures, franchise-policy conflicts, and fee complaints into explicit escalation packets with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Food-safety, illness, and fraud stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported actions and escalate whenever allergen exposure, contamination allegations, employee illness concerns, suspicious identity, or refund-reroute signals change the safe answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Handoff, closure, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm what counts as resolved versus handed off, record the next owner, and preserve public-baseline constraints when franchise-specific service models, delivery-platform rules, or local food-code adoptions are applied.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: orders, checks, receipts, reservations, guest profiles, and contact history
KDS timestamps, POS events, order-management events, curbside or delivery handoff records, and service tickets
payment-tender history, refund status, stored-value or loyalty records, coupon or promotion records, and fee displays
menu-item, modifier, allergen, disclosure, substitution, and 86-list artifacts
inventory availability, remake or comp records, waste notes, and service-recovery records
food-safety complaints, illness allegations, contamination or allergen notes, fraud or loss-prevention notes, and escalation records
systems_of_record: service desk or complaint ticketing system

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, policy-aware next-step summaries, and guest communication without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required order, payment, menu, loyalty, promotion, booking, service-ticket, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, discretionary service-recovery approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or food-safety signoff.
An allergen, contamination, employee-illness, suspicious identity, or loss-prevention signal changes whether support can act directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, franchise, delivery-platform, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, comp, remake, or fee reversal that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, refund, loyalty, safety, or fraud controls without explicit authority.
Authorize continued consumption or claim food is safe when the case is food-safety-sensitive.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a refund, remake, or comp when POS, order, or policy records do not support it
a payment mismatch is handled as routine guest service instead of being routed into the right billing-dispute or processor path
a support case closes without the minimum order, receipt, location, or payment evidence
repeat visit failures are treated as ordinary status checks instead of escalation-worthy complaints
support gives unsafe reassurance on allergen, contamination, or illness allegations
fraud or loyalty-account-takeover indicators are ignored in the name of convenience
the industry label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic customer service instead of franchise restaurant terminology and evidence handling

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local food-code adoption differences, franchise service-recovery matrices, delivery-platform contract rules, and stored-value or loyalty-program terms can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses California consumer-protection guidance plus CFPB, FDA, and FTC sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, local food-safety escalation paths, franchise policy, delivery-platform rules, and loyalty-program terms before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic guest-service scripting.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdictional and franchise-specific differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at support resolution and communication. Pricing overrides, discretionary compensation approval, fraud findings, store-operations remediation, and food-safety authority remain outside the lane.
